title: Phishing
description: Deceiving users into revealing credentials or installing malware via fraudulent emails, websites, or messages.
fullExplanation: Phishing is a social engineering attack that tricks victims into believing they're interacting with a legitimate entity. Email phishing sends mass fraudulent emails impersonating banks, services, or colleagues. Spear phishing targets specific individuals with personalized content. Vishing uses voice calls. Smishing uses SMS. The goal is credential theft, malware installation, or financial fraud. Phishing is the #1 initial access vector in breaches.

types.name: Email Phishing
types.description: Mass emails impersonating legitimate services — fake login pages, malicious attachments

types.name: Spear Phishing
types.description: Targeted attack on specific individual — personalized with victim's name, role, colleagues

types.name: Whaling
types.description: Spear phishing targeting executives (CEO, CFO) — high-value targets

types.name: Vishing
types.description: Voice phishing — phone calls impersonating IT support, banks, government

types.name: Smishing
types.description: SMS phishing — fake package delivery, bank alerts with malicious links

types.name: Clone Phishing
types.description: Duplicate legitimate email with malicious link/attachment replacing original

types.name: Business Email Compromise (BEC)
types.description: Compromise or spoof executive email to authorize fraudulent wire transfers

attackSteps.description: Research target via LinkedIn, company website, social media
Clone legitimate website, craft convincing email with urgency/fear
Send email with malicious link or attachment
Victim enters credentials on fake site — attacker captures them
Use stolen credentials for account takeover, lateral movement

keyFacts: 91% of cyberattacks start with a phishing email
Average cost of phishing attack: $4.65 million (IBM 2023)
DMARC/DKIM/SPF prevent email spoofing from your domain
MFA stops credential phishing — stolen password alone isn't enough
FIDO2/WebAuthn is phishing-resistant — key bound to legitimate domain
Homograph attacks: use Unicode lookalike characters in domain names

defenses.name: MFA / FIDO2
defenses.description: Even if credentials stolen, MFA prevents account takeover

defenses.name: Email Filtering
defenses.description: Spam filters, sandboxing attachments, link scanning

defenses.name: DMARC + DKIM + SPF
defenses.description: Prevent spoofing of your domain in phishing emails

defenses.name: Security Awareness Training
defenses.description: Regular phishing simulations and training for employees

defenses.name: Browser Warnings
defenses.description: Google Safe Browsing, SmartScreen flag known phishing sites
